hero_title: Who really thinks behind what you read?
hero_subtitle:
Human-Washing (passing AI-generated content off as human creation) blurs the line between authenticity and automation.
TCP/UP is a declarative protocol that simply indicates the role of humans and AI in content.
why_title: Binary 'AI or not AI' labeling is no longer enough
why_subtitle: From software proof to ethical declaration: bridging the gap between tool and reader
why_card1_text: The public doubts the origin of content. Without precise labeling, the value of human expertise dissolves into synthetic mass.
why_card2_text: AI is not all-or-nothing. It's a co-creation tool that requires a measurement scale true to the reality of editorial work.
why_card3_text: A user-powered protocol that transforms transparency into a guarantee of trust and credibility.
why_github_cta: Open project - contribute on GitHub
who_subtitle: TCP/UP is for everyone who creates, distributes or consumes digital content
who_card1_text: Bloggers, journalists, technical writers, consultants, artists, teachers… Anyone who wants to value their work and prove their human involvement, regardless of the level of AI assistance.
who_card2_text: For annual reports, official communications, training materials: displaying a TCP/UP label means improving the clarity of your editorial practices and complementing a documentary transparency approach.
who_card3_text: Media, academic journals, CMS can integrate TCP/UP as a transparency standard for their authors, with a simple and immediately understandable signature.
who_card4_text: At the other end of the chain, the public has a right to transparency. With the labels, everyone knows quickly whether they're reading 100% human opinion, assisted correction, or co-creation with AI.
who_footer_note: TCP/UP is an open, free tool based on good faith. Within everyone's reach.
who_registry_paragraph: See the registry of adopters
charter_subtitle: The TCP/UP charter (Transparent Content Protocol / User-Powered) defines a declarative protocol aimed at making visible the degree of human and algorithmic involvement in digital content production.
charter_card1_desc: It reassures about process rigor. We don't work in the fog.
charter_card1_point1: T: Transparency - The commitment to reveal the true source of work
charter_card1_point2: C: Content - The heart of the message: textual, visual or intellectual
charter_card1_point3: P: Protocol - A framework of strict rules to classify AI usage
charter_card2_desc: It empowers the creator. AI boosts productivity without replacing spirit.
charter_card2_point1: UP: User-Powered - Humans remain at the center of decision, AI as a performance lever, not substitution
charter_note: * Transparent Content Protocol / User-Powered translates in French as « Protocole de Transparence des Contenus / Piloté par l'Utilisateur ».
charter_read_full: Read the full charter (PDF) - coming soon
pillars_title: The 4 pillars of the TCP/UP charter
pillars_subtitle: The ethical pillars of the standard: a trust contract for the digital age
pillars_card1_text: The human is the sole architect of meaning. Artificial intelligence can assist, optimize or rephrase, but must never alone define the intellectual direction, key ideas or logical structure of content labeled "Human" (H). The initial spark and final validation belong to the mind.
pillars_card2_text: Assistance must never be hidden. As soon as AI influences wording, style or structure, the chosen label must honestly reflect this collaboration. Transparency is not an admission of weakness; it is proof of respect for the reader and a guarantee of sincerity.
pillars_card3_text: The author is the sole guarantor of their words. The use of generative tools never absolves the creator of ethical, legal and factual responsibilities. Whether assisted 5% or 95%, the human who applies their label assumes full authorship of the message and its consequences.
pillars_card4_text: Each label is an binding declaration that the author must be able to explain in good faith. If questioned, they must be able to justify the split between Substance (Content) and Form, making labeling a thoughtful act rather than a mere formality.
labels_subtitle:
From pure craftsmanship to automation: choose the cursor of your truth.
Need help? Find your label in 30s with the choice wizard.
labels_huc_desc: Content declared as human in origin, without resorting to AI tools for ideas, structure or formatting.
labels_huc_philosophy: "Content declared as human in origin, without resorting to AI tools for ideas, structure or formatting."
labels_hca_desc: Content of human origin. AI is used solely for technical correction (spelling, grammar, syntax), without rewording or modifying the substance.
labels_hca_philosophy: "AI is my proofreader. It guarantees clean text without ever touching my substance."
labels_hce_desc: Intellectual substance of human origin, whose formulation or formatting has been assisted by AI.
labels_hce_philosophy: "AI is my craftsperson. It shapes the raw material I provide to give it its final form."
labels_ace_desc: Co-construction: AI contributed to the substance of the content (plan, ideas, structure or wording), alongside the human.
labels_ace_philosophy: "It's teamwork. AI proposes a base, the human brings verification and the finishing touch."
labels_aic_desc: Content generated mostly or entirely by AI, human intervention limited to instruction, selection or distribution.
labels_aic_philosophy: "100% synthetic. The human defined the need, the machine executed the creation."
labels_deep_text1: Discover the logic that structures the 5 labels:
labels_deep_text2: Substance/Form distinction, H and A series, and the transparency philosophy that underpins them.
labels_footer_note: Quick memo: each use has its label.
ethics_subtitle: TCP/UP is a declarative protocol based on author sincerity, without third-party certification or technical proof.
ethics_card1_text: Each label is based on a sincere declaration by the author. No automated technical control: labeling engages the creator's moral responsibility and credibility.
ethics_card2_title: Neither proof nor certification
ethics_card2_text: TCP/UP is not a tamper-proof technical proof. It is a voluntary transparency approach, complementary to legal obligations without replacing them.
tagging_subtitle: Follow the TCP/UP protocol to guarantee total transparency to your readers
tagging_step1_desc: Evaluate the share of human intervention and AI assistance in your creation process.
tagging_step2_desc: Choose the TCP/UP label that fits (HUC, HCA, HCE, ACE or AIC) according to our classification grid.
tagging_step3_desc: Apply the visual seal to your support and insert the text signature [TCP/UP:XXX] in your metadata.
resources_subtitle: Integrate TCP/UP labels into your projects. Download the complete pack or access our badge library for custom integration.
resources_kit_desc: All logos, badges and official graphic charter as a ZIP file.
resources_lib_desc: Need a specific badge or ready-to-integrate metadata? Access all our variants, HTML codes and our metadata generator.
observatory_title: The protocol put to the test of reality
observatory_subtitle: Regular monitoring of AI transparency, regulation, practical cases and professional uses.
observatory_text: The blog extends the protocol to real-world contact: article analysis, critical readings, experience feedback and practical applications of TCP/UP.
